topic: In China employee termination: payroll risk-boundary position paper for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination payroll risk boundaries

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Defines framework for termination legality and compensation exposure.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports obligations tied to termination-month closure and statutory processing consistency.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Supports component-level treatment documentation and assumption logging.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Reinforces documentation discipline and process governance in multinational employer operations.

consensus: Termination payout decisions should be constrained by explicit risk boundaries.
Minimum release criteria reduce ad hoc high-risk decisions.
Boundary breaches should trigger escalation before payout, not after disputes emerge.
uncertainties: City-level evidence and dispute-handling practice can differ by facts.
Some treatment details need local confirmation before release.
English-language summaries may not capture all implementation nuance in Chinese sources.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Adopt explicit do-not-cross boundaries and a minimum viable release packet for in-cycle termination payouts.
